Four progressive 3-year-olds showed their class at Sandown and Rosehill on Monday, headlined by dominant victories, an eye-catching debut performance and continued improvement from well-bred gallopers on the rise through the grades.

Ballot heartbreak turned to Group 3 glory as Albury trainer Kym Davison celebrated his first city winner with Autumn Break, a $40,000 yearling whose pedigree, patience and placement combined for a breakthrough Randwick result.

The depth of quality continues to build ahead of The Chairman’s Sale on May 7, with Inglis confirming Group 2-winning juvenile Chayan (I Am Invincible) and fellow Group 2 winner Philia (All Too Hard) as the first supplementary additions to the catalogue.

Dual Group 1 winner Delacroix (Ire) will stand his first Southern Hemisphere season at Coolmore Australia in 2026 for $38,500 inc GST. By a Champion Sire out of a Champion race mare, Delacroix’s talent lived up to his incredible pedigree.

Godolphin’s 3-year-old crop has quietly become the story of the season, six individual Group 1 winners, doing it across sprint trips and Classic distances. From Green Spaces’ Derby breakthrough to Tentyris and Tempted at the top level, the results point to something deeper than a good run of horses. This is the outcome of a long-term approach built on patience, process and breeding with purpose.

Gun Runner had a massive day on Saturday in America with three Graded winners led by G1 Kentucky Derby hopeful Further Ado who won the G1 Blue Grass Stakes. In the other Derby trial, the G1 Santa Anita Derby, So Happy caused an upset.

A star filly on the rise, Sheza Alibi made it two Group 1s in succession with a stunnning G1 Doncaster Handicap victory. Proven mare Joliestar won her fifth Group 1 race in the TJ Smith to lead home a stakes double for Zoustar at Randwick whose Plaintiff remains unbeaten. Godolphin's Green Spaces won the Derby.

The focus of Saturday's electric juvenile action was squarely at Randwick, where Campione D’Italia won the G1 Sires' Produce Stakes in style after a strong Slipper finish, and Blue Door chalked up a new stakes winner for Stay Inside in the G3 Kindergarten Stakes. Out west, Snitzel made it a stakes double for the day with unbeaten Aurum Belle.
